Mount your TCloud as a network drive on: Mount your TCloud as a network drive on your computer.
TCloud can't open a network share from i: TCloud can't open a network share from inside the browser, so this extension pairs with a tiny bridge you run yourself. The bridge speaks WebDAV to your operating system and talks to TCloud on your behalf — every OS can mount a WebDAV drive natively, with no extra software.
Run this where the bridge should live —: Run this where the bridge should live — your Raspberry Pi is perfect. It needs Node.js 18+ and no extra packages.
On Windows, allow Basic auth over HTTP o: On Windows, allow Basic auth over HTTP once (see the README) or the drive won't connect.
SMB needs the full Samba protocol stack,: SMB needs the full Samba protocol stack, a privileged port and per-OS credential quirks. WebDAV is plain HTTP, maps cleanly onto TCloud's file API, and Windows, macOS, Linux and phones all mount it out of the box.
Keep the bridge on your local network an: Keep the bridge on your local network and protect it with a password — it serves your decrypted files.
